title: Martha Harrison
content:
Partner | Co-Head, International Trade and Investment Law
People.ContactEmail mharrison@mccarthy.ca
- University of Toronto
- University of Windsor
Martha Harrison is a partner and Co‑Head of the International Trade & Investment Law Group at McCarthy Tétrault LLP, where she also leads the firm’s Advertising & Marketing law practice. Widely regarded as a national leader on Canadian trade policy, Martha advises at the intersection of international trade, regulatory law, and strategic global investment, helping clients navigate legal risk in an increasingly complex and geopolitically driven global economy.
Martha is a frequent media commentator, prolific author and sought‑after speaker on trade policy, tariffs and regulatory developments affecting cross‑border commerce. She is regularly interviewed by national and international media on the commercial implications of trade disputes, sanctions and shifting geopolitical alliances, and is known for translating policy developments into clear, actionable business insight.
In an increasingly integrated global market, Martha’s hybrid practice in international trade and product regulatory law enables her to advise clients on how trade and regulatory risk affects complex commercial transactions, enterprise valuation, due diligence and cross‑border investment flows. She works closely with boards, senior executives and deal teams to assess and manage trade exposure in M&A transactions, joint ventures, supply and distribution arrangements and foreign investment, helping clients structure transactions that allocate risk appropriately while protecting value and facilitating capital movement.
Martha regularly advises international clients entering the Canadian market and Canadian companies pursuing outbound investment and global expansion, with a particular focus on how tariffs, sanctions, trade remedies, procurement regimes and regulatory compliance impact transaction structure, pricing, timing and long‑term investment strategy. Her practice spans a broad range of industries, including retail and consumer products, food and agri‑food, power and energy, mining and metals, oil and gas, and technology.
Martha’s international trade and investment practice covers the full spectrum of trade law, including customs, trade remedies, import and export controls, economic sanctions, controlled goods, treaty and free‑trade‑agreement compliance, public procurement, and investor‑state arbitration. She routinely represents clients before Canadian and international regulators and appears at all stages of trade disputes, from investigations through to litigation and arbitration.
Complementing her trade practice, Martha is a trusted advisor on product regulation and advertising and marketing law, advising on regulatory frameworks that affect market entry, commercialization and risk allocation. Her work includes guidance on product standards, packaging and labelling, transportation and importing/exporting requirements, as well as marketing, advertising, and distribution rules for regulated products, including foods and agri‑food products, cosmetics, natural health products, medical devices and over‑the‑counter drugs.
An experienced and highly respected member of the trade bar, Martha is known for her strategic, commercially grounded approach, helping clients align legal risk management with broader business, investment and growth objectives.
Martha’s leadership is reinforced by her academic and public service credentials. She is a former adjunct professor of international arbitration at Queen’s University and a former MBA sessional instructor at the DeGroote School of Business. She was also selected by the Government of Canada to serve on the roster of arbitrators under the Canada-United States-Mexico Agreement.
Martha is consistently recognized by all leading legal directories. She is ranked Band 1 in International Trade/WTO by Chambers Canada and Chambers Global, and is also recognized by Chambers for her work in Advertising & Marketing, Regulatory law, and Agri‑Food. Earlier in her career, she was named one of Lexpert’s Rising Stars: Leading Lawyers Under 40 (2013). In 2026, she was named Canadian Lawyer’s Female Trailblazer of the Year (Private Practice) in recognition of her leadership and influence in shaping Canada’s international trade and regulatory landscape.
Martha holds an LL.M. in international trade from the University of Toronto, an LL.B. from the University of Windsor, and an M.A. and B.A. from York University. She was called to the Ontario Bar in 2002.
